columns.JobTicketReference: Unique identifier for an individual job ticket (use for deduplication).
columns.WorksOrderRaisedDate: Timestamp when the works order was raised.
columns.WorksOrderIssuedDate: Timestamp when the works order was issued.
columns.WorksOrderDescription: Free‑text description of the requested works (may include tenant phone numbers).
columns.WorksOrderStatusDescription: Lifecycle state of the works order (e.g., Complete, Closed). Complete = operative finished works; Closed = financials completed.
columns.FullAddress: Property address associated with the works order.
columns.WorksOrderTargetDate: Target date/time assigned for completing the works order.
columns.ScheduledAppointmentStart: Start time of the scheduled appointment window.
columns.ScheduledAppointmentEnd: End time of the scheduled appointment window.
columns.WorksOrderReportedCompletedDate: The date when all job tickets under the works order have been completed.
columns.OperativeWhoCompletedJob: Name of the operative who completed the job.
columns.ArrivedOnSite: Operative tablet click to mark arrival on site.
columns.CompletedVisit: Operative tablet click to mark visit completion (times may be very close to ArrivedOnSite).
columns.NoAccess: Reason access was not possible (None implies access was granted).
columns.FollowOn: Flag indicating whether a follow‑on visit is required.
columns.FollowOnDescription: Free‑text reason for a follow‑on appointment.
columns.VisitDate: Timestamp when the operative accepts the job ticket on their tablet.
columns.FirstTimeFix: Indicator if job was completed on the first visit.
columns.SecondTimeFix: Indicator if job required a second visit.
columns.ThirdTimeFix: Indicator if job required a third visit

business_rules: Jobs completed: define as distinct jobs where WorksOrderStatusDescription ∈ {Complete, Closed}.
Status semantics: Complete = operative finished works; Closed = financials completed; the typical flow is Complete → Closed.
Scheduling window: JobTicketLinePlannedStartDate/EndDate together represent the allocated appointment slot assigned by the scheduling team.
Tablet event timing: ArrivedOnSite and CompletedVisit are operative tablet actions; some operatives may click through steps quickly so timestamps can be very close.
Deduplication rule: prefer JobTicketReference for removing duplicates. WorksOrderRef duplicates may be legitimate follow‑ons or second‑time fixes.
